q: Which mechanism prevents polyspermy after fertilization in humans?
options: The zona pellucida permanently blocks all sperm after one penetrates by dissolving its own receptors in a glucose-triggered reaction
A fast (electrical) block occurs immediately as membrane depolarization prevents further sperm binding; a slow block follows as cortical granules release enzymes that harden the zona pellucida
The nucleus of the first sperm releases a chemical that paralyzes all remaining sperm cells
Progesterone released by the fertilized egg causes uterine contractions that expel extra sperm
explanation: Immediately after sperm entry, the egg membrane depolarizes (fast block); then cortical granule exocytosis hardens the zona pellucida (zona reaction / slow block), preventing additional sperm from penetrating.
